name: Selection of Less-Secure Algorithm During Negotiation ('Algorithm Downgrade')
description: A system that negotiates security algorithms with another party selects a less-secure algorithm than the strongest mutually supported option, enabling attackers to exploit weaker cryptographic protections through protocol manipulation.
detection_pattern: EXTERNAL(peer_system) negotiates with TRANSFORM(algorithm_selection) but CONTROL node does not enforce minimum algorithm strength. Look for INGRESS(negotiation_request) flowing to TRANSFORM(algorithm_select) without CONTROL(minimum_strength_check) before TRANSFORM(crypto_operation).
